author: Afzal Hussain, M.
title: Gulshan-i-Firdaus
gloss: Verses in praise of the prophet Muhammad.
notes: Na't collection, 2nd ed. (Afzal Hussain again, cf. p37 s4) — the na't devotional-verse cottage industry.

title: Bahar-i-Chaman
notes: Ghazal/love-verse collection — the secular-lyric strand of Urdu poetry.

title: Bazm-i-Irfan, Hissa Dauom
gloss: Verses in praise of the prophet Muhammad.
publisher: Sheikh Abdur Rahim and Imam Bakhsh, Book-sellers

author: Bhatia, M. S.
title: Gauhar-i-Sukhan
notes: Love-verse collection by a Hindu poet (Bhatia).

author: Chiragh-ud-din, M.
title: Ahmaqon ka Photo
publisher: Sheikh Allah Bakhsh, Ilahi Bakhsh, Book-sellers
notes: 'Photograph of Fools' — satirical/admonitory verse; the 'photo' metaphor in the title signals how quickly photography entered popular idiom (cf. the photography manuals this quarter).

author: Diwan Chand, Ahsan
title: Foghan-i-Ahsan
gloss: A poem describing the benefits of life insurance.
notes: A poem promoting life insurance, distributed free — commercial/modern-financial instruction in verse form (the didactic-verse vehicle harnessed to the new insurance business); a striking modernity-in-poetry artefact.

author: Faryad, H. L.
title: Bhajan Faryad, Hissa-i-Awwal
notes: Anti-cow-killing bhajans (devotional verse) — the cow-protection (gau-raksha) movement in poetic form; a politically charged communal-mobilization theme (contrast the neutral 'Cow' nature-chart at p35 s29). The cow-protection agitation was a major flashpoint of Hindu-Muslim tension. Note 'bhajan' (Hindu hymn) in Urdu script.

author: Ghulam Nabi, M., Ghulami
title: Gulzar-i-Miran
gloss: Verses in praise of the prophet Muhammad and some saints of India.
notes: Na't plus praise of Indian Sufi saints — the local-saint-veneration extension of devotional verse; author based in Ahmadabad, Gujarat (out-of-province registrant).

title: Gulistan-i-Qawwali
notes: A qawwali (Sufi devotional-song) collection classed as 'love verses' — the qawwali genre in print; publisher is a Municipal Commissioner (Afzal Hussain), showing respectable-class patronage of qawwali. Relevant to the music thread (qawwali as a sung form).
